summary: The test suite thoroughly covers the core assignLabels function with 22 tests across 6 describe blocks: glob matching, multiple labels, priority ordering, edge cases, and real-world scenarios. Glob patterns, multiple labels per file, and priority ordering are well-tested with realistic rules. However, critical gaps exist: (1) no error handling tests (invalid patterns, malformed inputs, null values), (2) no tests for the CLI interface or file I/O (fixture/config loading), (3) missing boundary conditions (case sensitivity, special characters, very large inputs). The test organization is excellent with clear naming and logical grouping. Would catch most regressions in the core labeling algorithm, but would miss failures in error paths, CLI argument parsing, and file operations.
